definition: The current equity cycle is in a late-expansion phase with exact positioning obscured by fiscal support. The cycle has already turned for the median company but not for the cap-weighted index. Multiple frameworks converge on late-expansion or early-overshoot assessment.
evidence: Earnings revision breadth bifurcation: positive for mega-caps, negative for small-caps
Small-cap earnings in effective recession since mid-2023
macro_strategist_02 three-condition checklist: condition 3 (Minsky fragility) met, condition 2 (credit impulse) approaching threshold, condition 1 (fiscal floor) intact
Equal-weighted S&P 500 may already be in positive correlation regime while cap-weighted shows negative
open_questions: Whether fiscal floor can prevent the median company recession from reaching the index level
How long cap-weighted index can mask deterioration in the median company
Whether Minsky fragility condition triggers before or after fiscal impulse fades
